headline: Netflix Stock Has Lost Steam. This Next Step Could Heat Things Up Again.
summary: Netflix  is launching vertical videos to compete for engagement with social media companies, and this  J.P. Morgan  analyst thinks thats a solid plan.  Netflix  announced when reporting first-quarter earnings on April 16 that it will be launching vertical videos at the end of the month.  “We view vertical video as a way to engage users on another screen beyond the TV, and capture share of shorter, snackable moments, which could ultimately lead to increased engagement with NFLX’s longer-form content,” J.P. Morgan’s Doug Anmuth wrote in a research note.

headline: Netflix Is Being Misunderstood By Investors — Again. Why the 9% Dip Looks More Like an Opportunity Than a Warning
summary: Shares of Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX) dipped after clocking in some pretty respectable quarterly results. While the actual results were decent, investors sensed some hair on the quarter, and with a softer-than-expected guide, perhaps the swift 9% dip, which has since worsened to just over 14% since the latest results dropped, was warranted, especially considering the heated ... headline: Netflix Radford Studio Talks Add New Angle To Valuation Story
summary: Netflix is in advanced discussions to acquire the historic Radford Studio Center in Los Angeles. The deal would represent Netflix's first direct purchase of a major Hollywood studio lot. The potential acquisition signals a deeper commitment to in house production infrastructure and greater control over content creation. For investors watching NasdaqGS:NFLX, the Radford talks add a new angle to the story beyond the current share price of $92.58. The stock shows mixed recent performance, with...
